Duranta repens, commonly known as Durandeeldea urens, is a tropical flowering plant native to the Caribbean and Central America, characterized by its clusters of small, blue or white flowers and shiny leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Duranta repens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Duranta repens include teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any medical condition.
